title: tragacantha pygmaea
intro: Tragacantha pygmaea, also known as the dwarf goat's-thorn or dwarf gorse, is a small, deciduous shrub in the pea family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Tragacantha pygmaea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, although its efficacy and safety may vary depending on the source and preparation method.
